texto: Mom & pop stores are not about something small; they are about something big. Ninety percent of all U.S. businesses are family owned or controlled. They are important not only for the food, drink, clothing, and tools they sell us, but also for providing us with intellectual stimulation, social interaction, and connection to our communities. We must have mom & pop stores because we are social animals. We crave to be part of the marketplace.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Robert Spector sobre temas ligados a negocios. A afirmação “Mom & pop stores are not about something small; they are about something big. Ninety percent of a…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a negocios, economic-development, entrepreneurship, útil em momentos de entrepreneurship e small-business.
